title: The C3D Architecture
concept: C3D (Convolutional 3D) is a foundational architecture that uses homogenous 3x3x3 convolutions to learn spatiotemporal video features.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What two types of features must a video classifier extract?
exercises.explain: Yes! Video classifiers must capture spatial appearance and temporal motion.

cards.title: The VGG of video
cards.body: In 2D image classification, the VGG network became famous for its simple, uniform structure: stacking 3x3 convolutions over and over. When researchers wanted to build a simple, baseline 3D CNN for video, they did the exact same thing: stacking 3x3x3 convolutional cubes!

cards.title: C3D Network
cards.body: C3D (Convolutional 3D) is a foundational 3D CNN architecture. It processes 16-frame video clips using a series of 3x3x3 3D convolutions, 3D max pooling, and fully connected layers to output class probabilities.

cards.title: Toy building blocks
cards.body: Building a C3D model is like building a castle using only identical 3x3x3 Lego blocks. Instead of mixing different sizes of filters, keeping them uniform makes the model easy to construct and optimize.

cards.title: C3D configuration
cards.body: C3D uses 8 convolutional layers, 5 pooling layers, and 2 fully connected layers. All its convolutions are 3D with a kernel size of 3x3x3 and a stride of 1x1x1, ensuring smooth feature extraction.

cards.title: Not just for classification
cards.body: You might think C3D is only useful for predicting labels. In reality, C3D is widely used as a **feature extractor**! Engineers run a video through C3D, cut off the classification head, and save the 4096-dimensional output vector to describe the video's motion.

cards.title: 2D VGG vs. 3D C3D
cards.body: Comparing the architectures of VGG and C3D:

left.text:
Convolutions: 3x3
Inputs: (3, H, W)
Learns spatial image hierarchies

right.text:
Convolutions: 3x3x3
Inputs: (3, F, H, W)
Learns spatiotemporal video features

exercises.prompt: What is the standard convolutional kernel size used in the C3D architecture?
exercises.explain: Correct! C3D uses homogenous 3x3x3 convolutions throughout its network structure.

exercises.prompt: True or false: C3D can be used as a spatiotemporal feature extractor by removing the final classification layer.
exercises.explain: True. The internal representation of C3D captures dense spatiotemporal vectors that are useful for other downstream tasks.

exercises.sentence: C3D is modeled closely after the 2D ___ architecture, expanding its layers to support spatiotemporal volumes.
exercises.explain: C3D's design of uniform 3D convolutional stacks is directly inspired by the 2D VGG architecture.

exercises.prompt: Which input slice length is standard for training a C3D model?
exercises.explain: The C3D network is designed to process short video clips of 16 frames in length.

exercises.prompt: What is a major limitation of C3D compared to modern 2D classification networks like ResNet?

options.text: It has an enormous number of parameters and is highly prone to overfitting on small datasets.
It can only process grayscale videos with no color.
It does not support max pooling.
It outputs audio signals instead of classification labels.

exercises.explain: Exactly! 3D convolutions expand parameter sizes massively. Because C3D is deep and uses large fully-connected layers, it is very parameter-heavy and expensive to train. Next: how can we split space and time into two parallel streams? Next up: Two-Stream Networks!
